steps.title: Cool the burn
steps.instruction: Hold the burned area under cool (not cold) running water for 10–20 minutes immediately. Do not use ice, iced water, or any creams or greasy substances.

steps.title: Remove constrictions
steps.instruction: Gently remove rings, watches, belts, or tight clothing near the burned area before swelling begins.
steps.warning: Do not remove anything that is stuck to the burn.

steps.title: Cover the burn
steps.instruction: Cover the burn loosely with a sterile gauze bandage, cling film, or a clean plastic bag. Do not use fluffy cotton or anything that can stick to the wound.

steps.title: Manage pain
steps.instruction: Give paracetamol or ibuprofen to relieve pain if the person is conscious and it is not contraindicated. Follow dosage instructions carefully.

steps.title: Monitor for shock
steps.instruction: Keep the person warm and calm. Watch for signs of shock: pale or ashen skin, rapid shallow breathing, dizziness, or loss of consciousness. Lay them down and elevate legs if possible.
steps.warning: Do not give fluids to someone showing signs of shock.

doNot: Do not use ice, iced water, or creams on the burn
Do not burst any blisters that form
Do not remove clothing stuck to the burn
Do not use fluffy cotton wool directly on the burn

steps.title: Keep still
steps.instruction: Tell the person not to move the injured area. Support it gently in the position found using your hands or rolled clothing.

steps.title: Immobilize the injury
steps.instruction: Use padding such as clothing or towels around the injury to support it and prevent movement. Do not attempt to straighten or reposition the bone.
steps.warning: Never try to push a protruding bone back in.

steps.title: Apply cold compress
steps.instruction: Wrap ice or a cold pack in a cloth and apply to the injured area for up to 20 minutes to reduce swelling and pain. Never apply ice directly to skin.

steps.title: Elevate if possible
steps.instruction: If it is a limb injury and movement does not cause additional pain, gently raise the limb above heart level using a pillow or folded clothing.

steps.title: Treat for shock
steps.instruction: Have the person lie down on a firm surface. Keep them warm with a blanket. Do not give food or water as surgery may be required.
steps.warning: Call emergency services immediately for open fractures or spinal injuries.

doNot: Do not attempt to realign or straighten the bone
Do not move the person if a spinal injury is suspected
Do not apply a tourniquet unless there is severe uncontrolled bleeding
Do not give food or water

steps.title: Apply direct pressure
steps.instruction: Press firmly on the wound with a clean cloth, sterile pad, or bandage. Maintain continuous pressure for at least 10 minutes without lifting to check.

steps.title: Clean the wound
steps.instruction: Once bleeding slows, rinse gently under clean running water for 5–10 minutes. Carefully remove visible surface debris with clean tweezers if available.
steps.warning: Do not probe or irrigate deep puncture wounds.

steps.title: Apply antiseptic and cover
steps.instruction: Apply antiseptic ointment if available. Cover with a sterile bandage or clean non-fluffy cloth and secure with tape or a bandage.

steps.title: Elevate the area
steps.instruction: Raise the injured limb above heart level to reduce blood flow to the wound and slow bleeding.

steps.title: Monitor and replace dressing
steps.instruction: If blood soaks through the bandage, add more layers on top without removing the original dressing. Seek medical attention if bleeding does not stop within 20 minutes.
steps.warning: Seek emergency care for wounds with embedded objects — do not remove them.

doNot: Do not remove objects embedded in the wound
Do not close deep wounds with tape alone
Do not use fluffy cotton directly on the wound
Do not remove the original dressing if it soaks through

steps.title: Wash the bite thoroughly
steps.instruction: Wash the bite area immediately with soap and clean running water for at least 5 minutes. This is the single most important step to prevent infection.

steps.title: Control bleeding
steps.instruction: Apply gentle but firm pressure with a clean cloth if the bite is bleeding. Elevate the affected area above heart level.

steps.title: Apply cold compress
steps.instruction: Wrap ice in a cloth and apply to the bite for 10–15 minutes to reduce swelling, pain, and spread of venom if applicable.

steps.title: Check for allergic reaction
steps.instruction: Monitor closely for signs of anaphylaxis: hives, facial swelling, throat tightening, difficulty breathing, rapid pulse, or dizziness. This is a medical emergency.
steps.warning: Anaphylaxis can occur within minutes. Use an epinephrine auto-injector (EpiPen) if available and call emergency services immediately.

steps.title: Seek medical attention
steps.instruction: All animal bites require professional medical evaluation for rabies post-exposure prophylaxis and tetanus risk. Do not delay even if the wound appears minor.

doNot: Do not suck out venom from snake or insect bites
Do not apply a tourniquet for snake bites
Do not apply heat to insect stings
Do not delay seeking medical care for animal bites

steps.title: Rest the injured area
steps.instruction: Stop all activity immediately. Help the person sit or lie down in a comfortable position and avoid putting weight on the injured area.

steps.title: Apply ice
steps.instruction: Apply ice wrapped in a cloth or a cold pack to the swollen area for 20 minutes. Repeat every 2 hours for the first 48 hours.
steps.warning: Never apply ice directly to skin — always wrap in a cloth first.

steps.title: Compress the area
steps.instruction: Wrap with an elastic compression bandage starting below the injury and working upward. Wrap firmly but not so tight that it cuts off circulation.
steps.warning: Check that fingers or toes remain warm and pink. Loosen if they become numb or discoloured.

steps.title: Elevate
steps.instruction: Raise the injured limb above heart level using a pillow, cushion, or rolled clothing. Maintain elevation as much as possible for the first 24–48 hours.

steps.title: Pain relief
steps.instruction: Offer ibuprofen or paracetamol if available and not contraindicated. Ibuprofen also helps reduce inflammation. Follow dosage instructions.

doNot: Do not apply heat in the first 48 hours
Do not wrap the bandage too tightly
Do not bear weight if a fracture is suspected
Do not massage the injured area
